scope: ACL27 supplement pages 7-8 render evidence atlas and extended render-scene evidence view
root_cause: 4:3 proxy object renders were previously placed into very wide evidence tiles with center-cover/crop behavior, making proxy objects appear as clipped close-ups at paper scale.
changes: fig_supplement_render_atlas now uses four contained tiles per proxy row: MDL front, noMDL front, MDL detail, noMDL detail.
fig_supplement_render_scene_evidence_extended now pairs full-context proxy object views with contained material-detail views instead of direct crop-fit close-ups.
ACL supplement captions and sources.yaml were updated for the new front-context render sources.

local_visual_review.reviewer: Codex local visual review using render-visual-reviewer paper figure protocol; no subagent spawned because this environment only permits spawn_agent when explicitly requested.
local_visual_review.verdict: PASS for clipping/containment on pages 7 and 8; remaining caveat is that the white proxy objects are inherently low contrast, but they are no longer horizontally cropped by the composer.
local_visual_review.page_7: No hard crop visible; proxy front and detail views are contained in their frames; caption does not collide with the figure.
local_visual_review.page_8: Full-context and material-detail proxy views are both visible; page-scale figure and caption are contained.

commands_run: python -m pytest -q tests/test_paper_layout.py -k 'render_atlas or render_scene_evidence_extended or preserves_proxy_object_context or review_packet_contact_sheet'
follow_up_risks: Pages 22-23 and 30/36-38 remain high-density visual pages and should stay on the global review list.
The broader objective remains active: supplement-wide aesthetics and render density still need more visual iteration beyond this p7/p8 clipping fix.
